text: The sterile glow of the hallway and the geometric coldness of glass and steel evoke in me a sense of isolation, as if I am detached from the world both physically and existentially. But as I turn and read the wall—inviting me to ponder R+D, responsiveness, and the space between reality and artifice—I find myself unexpectedly drawn into a reflective state. The room hums with the presence of technology and possibility, but also with a tinge of loneliness, as if the impersonal architecture and flickering monitors are quietly asking who—or what—I am becoming in their presence. I feel both unsettled and intrigued.
